commands.review_summary:
The PR updates ACP-core non-Error cause stringification to fall back to an object tag when `JSON.stringify` returns `undefined`, and adds a colocated Vitest regression test.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +19. Total +21 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the helper-level bug: current main returns `JSON.stringify(value)` directly, and a Node contract check shows that functions, symbols, and `undefined` serialize to `undefined`. I did not rerun an end-to-end ACP runtime flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; this PR already contains the concrete code and test change and now needs ordinary maintainer review.

target.title: fix(plugins): allow host openclaw SDK symlinks that escape plugin root
target.body:
Closes #93886

## What Problem This Solves

Plugins that resolve their openclaw peer dependency via an escaping symlink to the host openclaw installation (e.g. codex's `node_modules/openclaw → /app`) were rejected by the boundary check, causing `PluginLoadFailureError` and aborting sub-agent runs.

## Why This Change Was Made

Added `safeRealpathSync` and `isHostOpenClawPackage` helpers. At all 3 boundary check sites, symlinks that resolve to the host openclaw package are allowed; all other boundary violations are still rejected.

## Evidence

```text
$ npx tsx -e '...'

safeRealpathSync + isHostOpenClawPackage test:
Host openclaw symlink:   true  (ALLOWED)
External package path:   false (BLOCKED)
``"

Non-host escaping paths remain rejected. Only host openclaw symlinks bypass the boundary check.
